danger_signals.visual_form: Imperial stormtroopers, battle droids, Dark Troopers in distinct armor silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Bulky power-armored silhouette, distinct from standard stormtroopers
danger_signals.meaning: Elite/high-threat enemy; Dark Trooper Project forces are present

danger_signals.visual_form: Toxic atmosphere areas, flowing rivers, lava, electrical traps
danger_signals.meaning: Health/shield damage; requires protective items or traversal skill
Player headlamp will reveal position to enemies; stealth risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Star Destroyer interiors, military architecture, tech-heavy environments

safety_signals.visual_form: Mission briefing locations, allied base interiors
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary respite; mission objectives communicated here

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating health packs, shield items, ammunition pickups, weapon upgrades
safety_signals.meaning: Resource restoration; player can prepare for upcoming threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright cone of light from player's perspective
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation aid, but signals player position in dark areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Ice cleats (equipment icon), air mask (equipment icon)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imperial insignia on uniforms and facilities; geometric Imperial aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard stormtroopers, Dark Troopers (power-armored variants), Imperial officers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Star Destroyer interiors, military bases, tech-heavy architecture, Imperial insignia on walls and doors

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rebel insignia in mission briefing sequences
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Safe houses, briefing locations, allied bases (not extensively shown in combat)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Project-specific markings on armor and facilities
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple Dark Trooper models; escalating threat levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Specialized research facilities, production areas on Coruscant and other Imperial locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Star Destroyer interiors, military bases, organized guard patterns, Imperial aesthetics dominating environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stormtroopers patrol in formation; they respond aggressively to player presence; bases are fortified with hazards and defenses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Appearance of increasingly advanced power-armored units; tech-heavy research facilities; specialized armor variants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dark Troopers behave more aggressively and intelligently than standard stormtroopers; represent escalating threat throughout missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mission briefing sequences; cutscenes revealing plot; Kyle Katarn's infiltration into restricted areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player objectives shift from recovery (Death Star plans) to investigation (Dark Trooper sources) based on world events

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Toxic atmosphere zones (visual haze), lava areas (orange glow), electrical traps (blue arcs), ice-covered surfaces (white/blue texturing)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player requires protective equipment; movement altered (ice cleats change traction); visibility and navigation affected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon racks, ammo caches, health stations at mission start; Jan Ors present in briefing sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can collect resources before infiltration; mission objectives explained via cutscene dialogue

environmental_storytelling.example: Star Destroyer interiors communicate Imperial military hierarchy through scale, materials, and geometric design; command decks vs. maintenance areas visually distinct
Imperial insignia on walls, doors, uniforms, and facility banners establish territory control and authority without dialogue
Toxic atmosphere zones on industrial planets, lava in volcanic areas, and electrical traps in tech facilities reflect world-specific dangers and context

Locations span Jabba the Hutt's yacht (criminal underworld), Coruscant computer vault (urban core), Star Destroyer (military command) — each location's visuals reinforce its role in the larger narrative
Ammunition and health packs distributed strategically; their placement suggests anticipated combat intensity and player navigation path
Heavily fortified areas with multiple stormtroopers signal important locations; sparse patrols in maintenance areas suggest secondary access routes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows predictable patrol routes; appears vigilant but routine
Aggressive engagement; calls for reinforcement; moves into combat formation
More intelligent tactics than standard stormtroopers; demonstrates advanced armor capabilities; higher threat priority
Visual wear on armor; reduced mobility or responsiveness signals vulnerability
Present in dialogue sequences; provides mission context and objectives; reinforces Rebel Alliance presence
Visible to enemies; stealth compromised; must choose between visibility and concealment
Continuous threat; player must equip protections or find alternate routes

notes: Dark Forces uses strong visual faction identity (Imperial white/grey vs. military interiors) and environmental storytelling to communicate world-state. Danger is signaled through enemy type escalation (stormtroopers → Dark Troopers), environmental hazards (atmospheric, thermal, electrical), and architectural context (fortified military installations). The game's progression reveals the Dark Trooper Project's threat level through increasingly sophisticated enemy encounters and mission objectives that shift from recovery to investigation. Safety is implied through power-up distribution, protected briefing areas, and equipment availability. The first-person perspective limits player awareness, making headlamp visibility and shadow placement critical to tension and danger signaling.
